name: Figs in Cabernet Sauvignon with Almond Ice C
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring milk to a boil. Place the sugar and egg yolks in a mixer or bowl and whip well. Add vanilla bean paste. Gradually add milk to the sugar and eggs while continuing to whip. Transfer the mixture to a large metal bowl and heat slowly. Using a wooden spatula, gently stir the egg yolks to prevent overcooking. Cool by placing the bowl in ice water.
2. Peel the figs, cut them in half, and sprinkle the tops with sugar. Place the fig halves in a flat dish with the wine (Cabernet Sauvignon or Port) and marinate overnight for at least 2-3 hours. Reserve the marinade and figs.
3. Pour the Creme Anglaise into a saucepan. Mix with 3/4 cups of almonds and pistachio paste, then process in an ice cream machine.
4. To serve, place the figs on plates. Shape the chilled Creme Anglaise into small spheres and add to the plates. Drizzle with marinade, add whipped cream mixed with sugar, and sprinkle with chopped almonds before serving.
